SEARCH
Package org.apache.arrow.datafusion.logical_expr

Class SimpleScalarUDF

java.lang.Object
org.apache.arrow.datafusion.logical_expr.SimpleScalarUDF
All Implemented Interfaces:
ScalarUDF
public final class SimpleScalarUDF extends Object implements ScalarUDF
A scalar UDF with a single fixed signature and return type.

Created via Functions.createUdf(java.lang.String, org.apache.arrow.datafusion.logical_expr.Volatility, java.util.List<org.apache.arrow.vector.types.pojo.ArrowType>, org.apache.arrow.vector.types.pojo.ArrowType, org.apache.arrow.datafusion.logical_expr.ScalarUDFImpl).

Example: 

import static org.apache.arrow.datafusion.Functions.*;

ScalarUDF doubleIt = createUdf("double_it", Volatility.IMMUTABLE,
    List.of(new ArrowType.Int(64, true)),
    new ArrowType.Int(64, true),
    (args, numRows, allocator) -> {
        BigIntVector input = (BigIntVector) args.get(0);
        BigIntVector result = new BigIntVector("result", allocator);
        result.allocateNew(numRows);
        for (int i = 0; i < numRows; i++) {
            result.set(i, input.get(i) * 2);
        }
        result.setValueCount(numRows);
        return result;
    });
ctx.registerUdf(doubleIt, allocator);
See Also:
  • Rust DataFusion: SimpleScalarUDF

  • Constructor Details

    • SimpleScalarUDF

      public SimpleScalarUDF(String name, Signature signature, List<org.apache.arrow.vector.types.pojo.ArrowType> inputTypes, org.apache.arrow.vector.types.pojo.ArrowType outputType, ScalarUDFImpl fn)
      Creates a new simple scalar UDF.
      Parameters:
      name - the function name as it will appear in SQL
      signature - the function signature (including volatility)
      inputTypes - the expected input Arrow types
      outputType - the output Arrow type
      fn - the function implementation
      See Also:

      Returns the name of this function as it will appear in SQL.

      Example: 

      @Override
public String name() {
    return "my_function";
}

      public Signature signature()
      Returns the signature of this function, including its volatility.

      Example: 

      @Override
public Signature signature() {
    return new Signature(Volatility.IMMUTABLE);
}

      signature in interface ScalarUDF

    • returnField

      public org.apache.arrow.vector.types.pojo.Field returnField(List<org.apache.arrow.vector.types.pojo.Field> argFields)
      Determines the return field (name + type) given the argument fields.

      Example: 

      @Override
public Field returnField(List<Field> argFields) {
    return Field.nullable("result", new ArrowType.Int(64, true));
}
      Specified by:
      returnField in interface ScalarUDF
      Parameters:
      argFields - the fields of the input arguments
      Returns:
      the field describing the return type
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: ScalarUDF::return_field_from_args

    • invoke

      public org.apache.arrow.vector.FieldVector invoke(List<org.apache.arrow.vector.FieldVector> args, List<org.apache.arrow.vector.types.pojo.Field> argFields, int numRows, org.apache.arrow.vector.types.pojo.Field returnField, org.apache.arrow.memory.BufferAllocator allocator)
      Invokes the function on the given input vectors.

      Example: 

      @Override
public FieldVector invoke(List<FieldVector> args, List<Field> argFields,
        int numRows, Field returnField, BufferAllocator allocator) {
    BigIntVector input = (BigIntVector) args.get(0);
    BigIntVector result = new BigIntVector("result", allocator);
    result.allocateNew(numRows);
    for (int i = 0; i < numRows; i++) {
        result.set(i, input.get(i) * 2);
    }
    result.setValueCount(numRows);
    return result;
}
      Specified by:
      invoke in interface ScalarUDF
      Parameters:
      args - the input vectors
      argFields - the fields describing each input argument
      numRows - the number of rows to process
      returnField - the expected return field
      allocator - the buffer allocator for creating the result vector
      Returns:
      the result vector
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: ScalarUDF::invoke_with_args

    • coerceTypes

      public List<org.apache.arrow.vector.types.pojo.Field> coerceTypes(List<org.apache.arrow.vector.types.pojo.Field> argFields)
      Performs type coercion on the input argument fields. The default implementation returns the argument fields unchanged.

      Example: 

      @Override
public List<Field> coerceTypes(List<Field> argFields) {
    return List.of(Field.nullable("arg0", new ArrowType.Int(64, true)));
}
      Specified by:
      coerceTypes in interface ScalarUDF
      Parameters:
      argFields - the fields of the input arguments
      Returns:
      the coerced fields
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: ScalarUDF::coerce_types
